mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in triggered instances show negative or flat performance despite concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong real economic activity, which correlates with higher corporate profits and positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 275) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to rising discount rates.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in CPIAUCSL > 275 periods show positive or flat performance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in triggered instances show negative or flat performance despite concurrent RSAFS and HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) increase borrowing costs and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in FEDFUNDS > 4.0% periods show positive or flat performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 0.3pp) signals labor market overheating, prompting inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in UNRATE < 0.3pp decline periods show positive or flat performance.
